# project conventions (CLAUDE.md):
# Tomorrow — Quick Start for Coding Agents

## Tech Stack
- **Nuxt 3** / **Nitro** / **Vue 3** SFC / **Prisma** / **Supabase** (PostgreSQL)
- **TypeScript** (ES2023, strict mode), **Tailwind**, **Chart.js**, **Zod** validation
- **Tests**: Vitest (run via `npm test`)
- **Node 22.x**

## Directory Map
| Dir | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| `pages/` | Vue SFC routes (nested = nested routes); `pages/app/` = authed, `pages/` = public |
| `server/api/` | Nitro API handlers; `requireAuth`/`requireVerifiedUser` guard all sensitive routes |
| `server/utils/` | Pure business logic + domain engines; **no I/O, no Date.now()** |
| `server/tasks/` | Nitro cron/scheduled-task handlers (also in vercel.json) |
| `server/plugins/` | Nitro server plugins (KV hydration, DB checks) |
| `components/` | Reusable Vue components; `components/app/` = main UI, `components/ui/` = design system |
| `composables/` | Vue composables (hooks); `useFetch`, `useAuth`, domain-specific |
| `prisma/` | Schema + migrations; **migrations are versioned + checksummed** (never edit applied migrations) |
| `scripts/` | Bash/Node tooling: linters, audits, provisioning (NOT run autonomously) |
| `types/` | Shared TypeScript types |

## Build / Test / Lint
```sh
npm run dev                  # Nuxt dev server (port 3000); env vars from .env
npm run build               # Prod build (runs migrations first if DATABASE_URL set)
npm test                    # Vitest all tests (runs both pure + DB-backed)
npm run lint:migrations     # Verify migration names vs schema (catches naming bugs)
npm run lint:syntax         # Check all .ts/.js syntax (no ts exec)
npx tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck  # Type-check entire project
npx vitest run --config vitest.pure.config.ts  # Pure tests only (no DB)
```

## Code Conventions (Observed, Not Enforced)
- **DB names**: snake_case tables (`@@map("live_clause_edits")`), camelCase columns quoted (`"roomId"`, `"bridgeStatus"`)
- **Code**: camelCase identifiers; PascalCase classes/types; CONST_CAPS for immutable config
- **Migration names**: `20260603000000_feature_name` (timestamp + kebab-case slug); immutable once applied
- **Prisma models**: match schema names; use `@map("col")` on fields only if table uses `@@map`; add `@db.Timestamptz(6)` for dates, `@db.Uuid` for UUIDs
- **Pure functions**: no `Date.now()`, no `Math.random()`, no file I/O—pass time/seed via params
- **Comments**: only document WHY (non-obvious invariants, workarounds); don't comment WHAT
- **Tests**: assert +

issues: Branch has persistent rebase conflicts after multiple attempts - needs manual intervention before implementation can proceed
Repository branch/worktree may be orphaned or missing based on repair directive
No specification of which existing uncertainty utility to model after - requires codebase search first
Mathematical specification incomplete: no formula given for 'analytic for ratios' or MC details (samples, seed strategy, convergence)
Input uncertainty data format and source not specified
Unclear what 'additive only' means - variance propagation semantics vs code mutation semantics
Interface of 'separate helper' for exposing uncertainty not defined
No specification of which metrics use analytic vs Monte-Carlo - requires understanding metric calculator layer
Build environment status unknown - may have dependency or config issues
suggestions: Start with git state inspection: check local branches, worktrees, and assess rebase conflict severity before coding
Search codebase for existing uncertainty/propagate patterns (grep: uncertainty, propagate, variance, p10, p90) to establish reuse target
Define TypeScript types for input (uncertainty per metric) and output (value + [p10,p90]) before implementing calculations
Document mathematical approach: ratio uncertainty formula, MC sample count, seed pattern, and percentile extraction method
Verify build environment works: run `npm install && npm run build` and `npx vitest --version` before implementation
Define which calculators need uncertainty and update their signatures as needed (clarify integration points)
Create minimal test fixtures covering: zero variance → point, small variance → small band, large variance → large band
Consider lightweight seedable RNG if no existing utility found - keep it PURE and deterministic
If rebase conflicts recur, consider squash-rebasing against current main to reset branch state
missing_context: Current git branch/worktree state and rebase conflict details
Location and API of existing uncertainty/propagation utilities in codebase
Input data schema: which metrics carry uncertainty, how is it represented
Output interface specification for value + [p10,p90] format
Metric calculator interfaces and their current signatures
Mathematical specification: ratio uncertainty formula, MC sample size, seed strategy
Build system status (npm, vitest, tsconfig working state)
Definition of 'separate helper' for uncertainty exposure
Clarification: which calculators need analytic vs Monte-Carlo
ai_review.merge_risk_reason: Branch has persistent rebase conflicts (4+ failed attempts), worktree/branch may be orphaned, integration into metric calculator layer risks breaking consumers, and incomplete mathematical/interface spec may lead to false starts. Requires manual rebase resolution and environment repair before coding can begin.

coverage_areas: Basic uncertainty propagation with zero and non-zero variance
Band calculation with proper p10/p90 bounds
Monte Carlo VaR/SCR calculation with seeded randomization
Edge cases: very small/large values, negative inputs, invalid percentiles
Band width properties and monotonic increase with stddev
Confidence level calculation from percentile bounds
Seeded randomization for Monte Carlo reproducibility
Analytic vs Monte Carlo method consistency
